create.admin_info.description: Please provide an email address to make sure you can edit or update this open letter.
create.faq.q1: How to create an open letter?
create.faq.q1.response: We recommend you to start a google doc with one or two other people. It's a co-creation process! Once you are happy with it, copy paste it here, publish it and share the URL with your collaborators to be the first to sign it.
create.faq.q2.response: An open letter is a great way to ask something to your company, organization, institution, city, government. Make sure that it is signed by people that are relevant to what you are asking.
create.faq.q3: Can I limit who can sign it?
create.faq.q3.response: No. That's why we recommend you to carefully share it to the right people at the beginning, so that they will get the opportunity to be first people showing up as the co-signatories.
create.text:
Copy paste or write your open letter here.

Pro tip: you can use the markdown syntax to format your text with **bold** or *italic*.
create.title: Title
error.alreadysigned: you already signed this open letter
error.server: The server is momentarily unreachable. Please try again later.
faq.q1.response: It's a public letter that you write to address together your company, organization, institution, city, government.
faq.q2: What is the difference with a petition?
faq.q2.response: The goal of a petition is to have as many people as possible to sign it. The goal of an open letter is to have people relevant to topic to sign it. It's not the same to have a petition to ask our government to make wearing a mask mandatory than having an open letter signed by 100 doctors, or a petition to ask Google to stop supporting the fossil fuel industry than an open letter signed by a 1000 of their employees. There is no author to an open letter, only co-signatories. No email address is collected. Who wrote it doesn't matter. Who signed it does.
faq.q3: Why do I need to provide an email address to sign a letter?
faq.q3.response: To avoid abuse. We want to make sure that each signature is linked to an actual email address. We don't record your email address unless you explicitly opted in to receive updates about the open letter that you are signing.
faq.q4.response: We have no cookies, no trackers, no 3rd party script or ads. We only ask for your email address to confirm your signature (see question above).
faq.q5: Can I limit who can sign it?
faq.q5.response: No. That's why we recommend you to carefully share it to the right people at the beginning, so that they will get the opportunity to be first people showing up as the signatories.
faq.q6: How to provide feedback or request a feature?
faq.q6.response: Start a conversation on our collective.
faq.q7.response: You can contribute contribute code on Github 🧑🏻‍💻 or you can make a financial contribution to our collective 💰. Thank you for your support! 🙏
faq.q8: How to remove or edit my signature?
faq.q8.response: In your confirmation email, you have a unique link to edit your signature. Keep it safely for future use (and don't share it with anyone!). For older signatures (before May 2025) or if you have lost your confirmation email, you can use this Google Form.

pricing.title: Simple pricing for impactful letters
pricing.subtitle: Public letters are always free. Need to ensure every signature is genuine? Choose invitation-only.
pricing.public.title: Public
pricing.invite.title: Invitation Only
pricing.invite.feature2: Personal invitation links — web of trust
pricing.invite.feature4: Stop AI bots and fake signatures
pricing.why.title: Why invitation-only?
pricing.why.bots.title: Stop AI bots
pricing.why.bots.desc: AI can generate thousands of fake signatures. Invitation-only letters ensure every signer is a real person, invited by someone you trust.
pricing.why.genuine.title: Genuine signatures only
pricing.why.genuine.desc: Every signature comes from a person who was personally invited. No drive-by signing, no manipulation.
pricing.why.weight.title: More weight for your letter
pricing.why.weight.desc: 100 verified, invited signers carry more weight than 10,000 anonymous ones. Decision-makers take invitation-only letters more seriously.
pricing.why.trust.title: Web of trust
pricing.why.trust.desc: See who invited whom. Each signer can invite others, creating a transparent chain of trust that strengthens your letter.
home.hero.title: Create an open letter and sign it together
home.hero.subtitle: A free, open civic tool for collective action. No cookies, no trackers. 3,600+ open letters, 490,000+ signatures.
home.invite.title: Invitation-only letters
home.invite.subtitle: Need to make sure every signature is genuine? Limit who can sign with personal invite links or email domain restrictions.
home.invite.bot.title: Stop AI bots
home.invite.bot.desc: AI can generate thousands of fake signatures in seconds. Invitation-only letters ensure every signer is a real person.
home.invite.trust.title: Web of trust
home.invite.trust.desc: Each signer can invite others. See who invited whom — a transparent chain that strengthens your letter.
home.invite.weight.title: More weight
home.invite.weight.desc: 100 verified, invited signers carry more weight than 10,000 anonymous ones.
home.step1.title: Write your letter
home.step1.desc: Create your open letter in one or multiple languages. Add images and format with markdown.
home.step2.title: Choose public or invitation-only
home.step2.desc: Public is free and open to everyone. Invitation-only (€10) lets you control who can sign.
home.step3.title: Share and collect signatures
home.step3.desc: Share the URL or send personal invitations. Signatures are verified via email or passkey.
home.step4.title: Make an impact
home.step4.desc: Send your letter to decision-makers with verified signatures that carry real weight.
faq.q3.response: Yes! With our invitation-only letters (€10), you can control exactly who signs your letter using personal invitation links or email domain restrictions. Public letters remain free and open to everyone.
faq.q5.response: Yes! With our invitation-only letters (€10), you can control exactly who signs your letter using personal invitation links or email domain restrictions. Public letters remain free and open to everyone.
